basics.name: Maximilian Raff
basics.summary:
Currently employed at https://www.iams.uni-stuttgart.de/institute/team/Raff/. Dr.-Ing. in Robotics
System Theory · Mechanics · Automation · Learning

publications.name: Exploring soft contact models in the optimization of gaits
publications.summary: Master's thesis on the role of compliant contact models in simulation and gait optimization for legged robotic systems.

publications.name: Connecting gaits in energetically conservative legged systems
publications.summary: A nonlinear-dynamics view of conservative gait families, with continuation methods for identifying connected gait manifolds and bifurcation structure.

publications.name: Generating families of optimally actuated gaits from a legged system's energetically conservative dynamics
publications.publisher: 2022 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
publications.summary: A homotopy-based method that maps passive conservative gaits to locally optimal actuated motions for more realistic legged models.

publications.name: Hard vs. soft contacts in trajectory optimization of legged robots: An example with a compliant rubber foot
publications.summary: A workshop paper comparing rigid and compliant contact models for simulation accuracy and optimization behavior on legged robotic hardware.

publications.name: An approach for generating families of energetically optimal gaits from passive dynamic walking gaits
publications.publisher: 2023 IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
publications.summary: A continuation-based method for generating libraries of locally optimal bipedal gaits by starting from passive dynamic walking solutions.

publications.name: Swing-up of a weakly actuated double pendulum via nonlinear normal modes
publications.summary: A nonlinear-normal-mode approach for swinging up a double pendulum with very limited actuation authority.

publications.name: The indirect method for generating libraries of optimal periodic trajectories and its application to economical bipedal walking
publications.summary: A preprint formalizing indirect trajectory optimization for periodic gait libraries and comparing it against direct methods on economical bipedal walking.

publications.name: Koopman based trajectory optimization with mixed boundaries
publications.summary: A paper on partial convexification of trajectory optimization problems with mixed boundary constraints using Koopman-operator ideas.

publications.name: The impact of AI on engineering design procedures for dynamical systems
publications.summary: A broad perspective paper on where AI methods can assist modern engineering design workflows for dynamical and mechatronic systems.

publications.name: Continuation and optimization of gaits and other non-smooth orbits
publications.summary: PhD thesis on mathematical tools that connect economical periodic motions, continuation methods, and morphology-aware design in legged systems.

publications.name: How Leg Stiffness Affects Energy Economy in Hopping
publications.publisher: The 12th International Symposium on Adaptive Motion of Animals and Machines (AMAM2025) and 2nd LokoAssist Symposium 2025
publications.summary: A conference paper examining how leg stiffness shapes hopping energy economy and compares variable-stiffness and fixed-stiffness designs.

publications.name: Data publishing in mechanics and dynamics: challenges, guidelines, and examples from engineering design
publications.summary: A collaborative article on best practices for publishing datasets in mechanics and dynamics research.

publications.name: Continuation of periodic orbits in conservative hybrid dynamical systems and its application to mechanical systems with impulsive dynamics
publications.summary: A journal article extending continuation ideas to conservative hybrid systems with impulsive dynamics and illustrating them on mechanical examples.

publications.name: Discovering Optimal Natural Gaits of Dissipative Systems via Virtual Energy Injection
publications.summary: A framework for finding passive-like motions in dissipative systems and continuing them into energy-optimal actuated gaits.

presentations.name: Talk 1 on Relevant Topic in Your Field
presentations.event: UC San Francisco, Department of Testing

presentations.name: Tutorial 1 on Relevant Topic in Your Field
Talk 2 on Relevant Topic in Your Field
Conference Proceeding talk 3 on Relevant Topic in Your Field
presentations.event: Testing Institute of America 2014 Annual Conference

teaching.description: Master's course on multibody kinematics and dynamics for robotics, mechatronics, and biomechanics, taught repeatedly at the University of Stuttgart.

teaching.course: Dynamics and Control of Legged Locomotion
teaching.description: Master's course on the dynamics and control of legged systems, from biomechanics and nonlinear dynamics to optimal control and hands-on programming.
Master's course on elastostatics and beam theory, taught as Technische Mechanik II, with additional written-examination support for related mechanics courses.

teaching.course: Finite Element Methods (Methode der Finiten Elemente in Statik und Dynamik)
teaching.description: Master's course on the finite element method for statics and dynamics, covering variational foundations, element formulation, assembly, and numerical solution.

teaching.course: Introduction to Engineering Cybernetics (Einführung in die Technische Kybernetik)
teaching.description: Bachelor's course introducing modeling, simulation, systems theory, and control through simple examples as a foundation for later advanced courses.
Bachelor's introduction to rigid-body kinematics and statics, covering equilibrium, trusses, beams, centers of mass, and basic deformation.
Selection of supervised Bachelor's, Master's, and study projects, with public University of Stuttgart OPUS records where available.
